features.summary: Broadcom reported consolidated net revenue of $12.5 billion for Q2 FY2024, a 43% increase year-on-year, driven by a full quarter of VMware contributions. Excluding VMware, revenue grew 12% year-on-year, primarily due to a 280% increase in AI revenue, which reached $3.1 billion. The infrastructure software segment saw revenue of $5.3 billion, up 175% year-on-year, while semiconductor revenue was $7.2 billion, up 6% year-on-year.
features.management_outlook: Broadcom raised its fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance to $51 billion, with expectations for AI revenue to exceed $11 billion. The company anticipates continued strength in AI and a modest recovery in non-AI semiconductor revenue in the second half of the year. VMware's integration is progressing well, with expectations for its revenue run rate to reach $4 billion per quarter by fiscal Q4 2024.
features.risks: The company highlighted potential risks related to cyclical weakness in semiconductor revenue, particularly in the broadband and server storage segments, which are expected to decline year-on-year. Additionally, Broadcom faces competition in the AI and networking markets, particularly from companies like NVIDIA, which could impact market share and pricing strategies.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of growth include the strong demand for AI-related products and services, with networking revenue expected to grow 40% year-on-year. The successful integration of VMware and the transition to a subscription licensing model are also significant contributors. Furthermore, Broadcom's established relationships with hyperscalers and its unique product offerings in networking are expected to drive continued revenue growth.
